title: combretum contractum
intro: Combretum contractum, also known as the East African bushwillow, is a plant species that belongs to the Combretaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Combretum contractum include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve the leaves and bark being dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
